commit.message:
fix: plug memory leaks in MFA tracker, computed field cache, permission cache (#1073)

- MfaAttempts: add LastActivityUtc tracking + periodic scavenge (every 60s)
  that evicts entries idle >10 min. RegisterSuccess now removes the key
  entirely. Prevents unbounded growth from unique IP/user combinations.

- ComputedFieldService: add lazy scavenge (every 30s) that purges expired
  cache entries + hard cap at 10k entries. Previously expired entries
  accumulated forever in the ConcurrentDictionary.

- PermissionResolver: Invalidate() now clears the entire cache instead of
  only bumping the generation counter. Stale entries for inactive users
  previously accumulated indefinitely.

Co-authored-by: root <root@localhost.localdomain>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix sync loop not responding promptly to new files or force sync

The sync loop was calling time.sleep(sync_interval) unconditionally,
causing watchdog events and force sync signals to be ignored for up to
5 minutes (the default sync interval).

- Add threading.Event (_wakeup_event) to SyncDaemon
- Set the event in SyncEventHandler._queue_change so watchdog-detected
  file changes wake the sync loop immediately
- Replace time.sleep(sync_interval) with a 1-second polling loop that
  breaks early on _wakeup_event or force_sync_path signal file

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

against actual GAMS codebase found 18 mismatches (now 14 fixed, 4 annotated).

Wrong prefix fixes:
- p15_waste_scen → s15_waste_scen (scalar, not parameter)
- vm_nr_surplus → v50_nr_surplus_cropland (local, not interface)
- f22_conservation_fader → p22_conservation_fader

Wrong name fixes:
- pm_tau(t) → pcm_tau(j,tautype) (module_14)
- pm_carbon_density_secdforest → pm_carbon_density_secdforest_ac (module_29)
- vm_cost_wat → vm_water_cost (3 occurrences in module_42)
- ic42_pumping_cost replaces invented s42_pumping_cost (module_42)
- vm_demand → vm_supply (module_17)
- s30_rotation_scenario_speed → s30_rotation_scenario_start (module_30)

Invented names replaced:
- s13_tau_response → actual param reference (module_14)
- s14_yld_bioen_scaling → s14_carbon_fraction (module_14)

- vm_import/vm_export in module_21 → replaced with actual GAMS equations

commit.message:
fix: disable mmc1 (WiFi SDIO) in U-Boot DTS to fix eMMC boot

When mmc1 (mmc@1c10000, the BCM4330 SDIO host) is enabled in the U-Boot
DTS without MMC aliases, the block layer assigns devnums by DT scan order:

  mmc@1c0f000 (SD)   -> devnum 0
  mmc@1c10000 (WiFi) -> devnum 1  [steals the eMMC slot]
  mmc@1c11000 (eMMC) -> devnum 2

sunxi board.c maps BOOT_DEVICE_MMC2 (eMMC boot) to mmc_get_env_dev()=1,
so U-Boot looks for the FAT boot partition on 'mmc 1'. With WiFi bound as
devnum 1, every eMMC-boot attempt fails:

  Loading Environment from FAT... ** Bad device specification mmc 1 **

U-Boot then falls through to PXE and never accesses the eMMC boot
partition, making eMMC installs unbootable.

Fix: add patches/uboot/0002 which sets status = "disabled" on the mmc1
node in sun9i-a80-cubieboard4.dts. U-Boot does not use WiFi during boot.
With mmc1 removed from DM binding:

  mmc@1c0f000 (SD)   -> devnum 0  (BOOT_DEVICE_MMC1 -> mmc 0) [correct]
  mmc@1c11000 (eMMC) -> devnum 1  (BOOT_DEVICE_MMC2 -> mmc 1) [correct]

To apply: rm build/sources/u-boot-2024.01/.patched && scripts/build-uboot.sh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(wasm): downgrade Emscripten 3.1.50 -> 3.1.43 to match Qt 6.6.3 emval ABI

Root cause: Qt 6.6.3 was prebuilt with Emscripten 3.1.37. At Emscripten
3.1.44, the emval implementation was changed so that _emval_new,
_emval_call_void_method etc. moved from compiled WASM objects to
JavaScript-only. Qt 6.6.3's libqwasm.a still references these as WASM
symbols; wasm-ld cannot resolve them when linking against 3.1.50.

Fix: use Emscripten 3.1.43 — the last version with WASM-compiled emval,
which is binary-compatible with Qt 6.6.3's prebuilt objects. 3.1.37 itself
was removed from GCS storage; 3.1.43 is the nearest available version.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: network bandwidth shows N/A instead of 0 for undocumented SKUs

Two bugs caused old SKUs (Standard_D3, original D-series, etc.) to show
'0 Mbps' instead of 'N/A':

1. Azure Table Storage coerces None integer properties to 0 on write.
   Fix: skip adding networkBandwidthMbps to entity when value is None
   so the property is never written to storage for undocumented SKUs.

2. formatBandwidth(0) rendered '0 Mbps' - only guarded against null/undefined.
   Fix: treat 0 as no-data since no VM genuinely has 0 Mbps bandwidth.

Old SKUs without published bandwidth data (original D-series, Av1, etc.)
will now correctly display N/A in the UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: reduce parseCache complexity from 17.9 to 7.0

Split parseCache into focused helper functions to improve code quality and maintainability:
- validateCacheData: validates file size and magic header
- parseCacheHeader: reads cache file header structure
- parseCacheEntries: processes all cache entries into map
- shouldIncludeEntry: filters entries by architecture and library type

Results:
- Main function complexity reduced by 60.9% (17.9 → 7.0)
- All helper functions below complexity threshold (≤10)
- Zero test regressions - all existing tests pass
- Improved separation of concerns and readability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Route 6 vs Route 7 benchmark comparison handover

Comprehensive benchmark comparison across both question sets:
- Route 7 beats Route 6 on Q-D (55/57 vs 53/57) and Q-G (53/57 vs 52/57)
- Route 7 is 40-50% faster with 2x shorter responses
- Zero questions where Route 6 passes and Route 7 fails
- Deep dive on Q-G6 (shared blind spot) and Q-G10 (Route 6's only edge)
- Clarification: Route 7 does NOT use community summaries in synthesis

commit.message:
Add optional per-method token parameter (#11)

* Add optional per-method token parameter to all API methods

Allow all methods in both Gofile (async) and Sync_Gofile to accept an
optional `token` parameter that overrides the instance token. This
enables using a single client instance with multiple accounts without
reinitializing the class.

The token resolution logic in _api_request is:
  auth_token = token or self.token
  if need_token and auth_token is None: raise InvalidToken()
  if auth_token: headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {auth_token}"

Co-authored-by: Itz-fork <77770753+Itz-fork@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add error handling for invalid API responses

Handle exceptions when parsing API response.

* Update README.md docs and code examples for per-method token parameter

- Add explanation of per-method token override in initialization section
- Add per-call token examples to async and sync code blocks
- Add new Multi-Account Usage section with async and sync examples
- Update all method signatures in Docs section with token parameter

Co-authored-by: Itz-fork <77770753+Itz-fork@users.noreply.github.com>

* Bump version from v2.0.1 to v2.1

---------

commit.message:
fix: Coming Up Next retry logic + targeted playlist queries

Root cause identified: Coming Up Next only rendered during full redraws,
which only happen on track changes. The initial render fires before mpv
finishes loading the playlist, gets empty data, caches it, and never
retries.

Three fixes:

1. Targeted playlist queries instead of bulk fetch:
   Use _jukebox_fast_get 'playlist/N/filename' and 'playlist/N/id'
   to query individual entries. Small responses that socat handles
   reliably (vs the 38KB full playlist that broke both socat and
   python IPC).

2. Retry logic in the main loop:
   When _jukebox_last_next_file is empty, poll playlist-count and
   playlist-pos on each tick. Once mpv reports the playlist is loaded
   (next index < count), force a full redraw to populate Coming Up Next.

3. Don't cache empty results:
   Only set _jukebox_last_next_file when we get a real filename.
   Show 'Loading...' instead of 'End of playlist' when data isn't
   available yet but more tracks exist. Reset the cache on track
   change so Coming Up Next refreshes.

Also fixes: debug log timestamps use $SECONDS instead of date/cut
(avoids PATH issues when running via zsh -c).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: render `plantuml` fences as SVG via Kroki (fence_prefix correction) (#92)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct kroki fence_prefix to render ```plantuml``` blocks as SVG

The mkdocs-kroki-plugin defaults to fence_prefix="kroki-", requiring
```kroki-plantuml``` fences. Docs use ```plantuml``` (no prefix), causing
them to render as plain code blocks instead of SVG diagrams.

Fix: set fence_prefix="" so standard ```plantuml``` fences are processed.
Also set enable_mermaid=false to avoid intercepting mermaid blocks already
handled by pymdownx.superfences for client-side JS rendering.

Co-authored-by: stephanedenis <10110359+stephanedenis@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add journal entry for merge confirmation of plantuml fix

commit.message:
perf: eliminate reflection from per-request hot paths (#1057) (#1072)

* perf: eliminate reflection from per-request hot paths

- ActionExpander: cast to IBaseDataObject instead of GetProperty("Key")
- ServerLookupResolver: use DataScaffold metadata + cached accessors
  instead of GetProperties()/GetProperty() reflection
- ReportExecutor: cache BaseDataObject properties statically instead of
  per-call typeof(BaseDataObject).GetProperty()
- CalculatedFieldService: cache PropertyInfo[] per type in fallback path
- BinaryObjectSerializer: replace Activator.CreateInstance with compiled
  Expression.New factory delegates cached in ConcurrentDictionary
- ComputedFieldService: prefer DataScaffold metadata compiled getters
  over raw reflection in GetCachedGetter
- AuditService: cache PropertyInfo[] per type in static ConcurrentDictionary
- SearchIndexing: make _typeMetadata cache static to share across instances

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: replace remaining Activator.CreateInstance with compiled factories

- DataScaffold: replace 6 Activator.CreateInstance calls in
  ListFactoryCache/InstanceFactoryCache/DictFactoryCache with
  Expression.New compiled delegates via shared CompileFactory helper
- LocalFolderBinaryDataProvider: cache singleton flag property discovery
  per type instead of reflecting on every save

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

repository.name: neopz-docker
repository.description: Dockerized development environment for NeoPZ, a C++ finite element method library. Includes a multi-stage image with optional MUMPS, METIS, Intel MKL and Log4cxx support, interactive build configuration, volume-mounted project workflow, and VS Code Dev Containers integration.

commit.message:
🐛 fix(dockerfile): fix Stage 3 ARG scoping, path typo, apt install and MUMPS+MKL build

- Re-declare USING_LOG4CXX and USING_METIS as ARG in the runtime stage;
  Docker ARGs do not cross stage boundaries, so liblog4cxx-dev and
  libmetis-dev were never installed in the final image regardless of
  what was passed at build time.
- Replace the inline shell expansion in apt-get (which produced an
  empty string argument) with the set-variable-then-install pattern
  already used in Stage 2.
- Fix WORKDIR, VOLUME and mkdir path: neopz_projects -> projects_neopz,
  matching the mount point used by run.sh.
- Remove USING_MKL from Stage 1 and hardcode -DLAPACK_VENDOR=OpenBLAS
  for MUMPS. MKL is not installed in Stage 1, so passing LAPACK_VENDOR=MKL
  caused a CMake configure failure. MUMPS only needs a BLAS/LAPACK
  implementation; OpenBLAS is sufficient regardless of MKL being used
  by NeoPZ in Stage 2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: neopz-docker

commit.message:
🔧 chore(gitignore): replace whitelist pattern with conventional ignores

The previous approach (deny everything with *, then whitelist each file)
is unnecessarily strict for a repository that generates no local build
artifacts. Replace it with a conventional gitignore that only excludes
OS artifacts (.DS_Store, Thumbs.db), editor files (.vscode/, .idea/,
swap files) and local environment overrides (.env).

commit.message:
perf: replace ConvolveOp Gaussian blur with 3-pass box-blur approximation

Replace the O(kernel_size)-per-pixel ConvolveOp-based Gaussian blur with a
3-pass box-blur approximation that runs in O(1) per pixel using a sliding
window. Works directly on int[] pixel arrays, handles edge clamping, and
reuses existing temp buffers.

Fixes #121

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Transform to Solana x402 USDC payment MCP server

Replace the RugCheck MCP server with a Solana x402 payment server
that exposes 5 MCP tools for AI agents:

- get_wallet_info: Show wallet address, balances, network
- get_sol_balance: Check SOL balance for any address
- get_usdc_balance: Check USDC balance for any address
- send_usdc: Transfer USDC to a Solana address
- make_x402_payment: Fetch x402-protected resources with auto payment

Uses official Coinbase x402 SDK (@x402/core, @x402/svm, @x402/fetch)
for the x402 protocol flow and @solana/web3.js for direct operations.

Supports mainnet and devnet via SOLANA_NETWORK env var.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Bump minimatch, @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin and @typescript-eslint/parser in /backend (#41)

* Bump minimatch, @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin and @typescript-eslint/parser

Bumps [minimatch](https://github.com/isaacs/minimatch) to 10.2.4 and updates ancestor dependencies [minimatch](https://github.com/isaacs/minimatch), [@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/tree/HEAD/packages/eslint-plugin) and [@typescript-eslint/parser](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/tree/HEAD/packages/parser). These dependencies need to be updated together.


Updates `minimatch` from 9.0.3 to 10.2.4
- [Changelog](https://github.com/isaacs/minimatch/blob/main/changelog.md)
- [Commits](https://github.com/isaacs/minimatch/compare/v9.0.3...v10.2.4)

Updates `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` from 6.21.0 to 8.56.1
- [Release notes](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/releases)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/blob/main/packages/eslint-plugin/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Commits](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/commits/v8.56.1/packages/eslint-plugin)

Updates `@typescript-eslint/parser` from 6.21.0 to 8.56.1
- [Release notes](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/releases)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/blob/main/packages/parser/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Commits](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/commits/v8.56.1/packages/parser)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: minimatch
  dependency-version: 10.2.4
  dependency-type: indirect
- dependency-name: "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin"
  dependency-version: 8.56.1
  dependency-type: direct:development
- dependency-name: "@typescript-eslint/parser"
  dependency-version: 8.56.1
  dependency-type: direct:development
...

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>

fix(site): expand content width and fix hero layout clipping

- Override .md-content__inner max-width to 1100px (was ~44rem/700px)
- Add min-width: 0 to hero code panel to prevent grid blowout
- Allow horizontal scroll on code pre instead of clipping
- Use clamp() on stat numbers to prevent line wrap at mid viewports
- Step cards: minmax(180px, 1fr) to prevent over-narrow columns

commit.message:
fix: use raw cobertura files for code coverage summary (#33)

## Problem
The irongut/CodeCoverageSummary action was failing with:
> Parsing Error: No package data found

This happened because it was reading the merged Cobertura.xml produced
by ReportGenerator, which doesn't include the `<packages>` structure the
action expects.

## Changes
- Remove the intermediate ReportGenerator step (its HTML and merged
Cobertura outputs were not consumed by anything)
- Point irongut/CodeCoverageSummary directly at the raw per-test-run
`*.cobertura.xml` files via a glob pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(mobile): fix 4 startup crashes causing app not to open

main.dart:
- Add WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized() before runApp()
  Required for SharedPreferences and Geolocator plugin registration

api_service.dart:
- Wrap http.get() and http.post() in try/catch (SocketException on no network)
- Wrap jsonDecode in _handleResponse try/catch (FormatException on HTML error pages)
- All errors now return {success:false, error:...} map instead of throwing

auth_provider.dart:
- Add _isRestoring flag (true until _restoreSession() completes)
- Add try/catch around SharedPreferences read in _restoreSession()
- Expose isRestoring getter for SplashScreen to poll

splash_screen.dart:
- Replace bare Future.delayed with Future.wait([delay, _waitForRestore()])
- _waitForRestore() polls auth.isRestoring every 50ms until false
- Guarantees route decision made AFTER session token is loaded from disk

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: match cutready workflow structure exactly

- Remove CI workflow (no separate CI, matching cutready)
- Tests run only in release builds via npm test
- Add npm test script (Playwright + Rust tests)
- Use 'npm test' in release workflows (matching cutready)
- Release-please → triggers release-win-linux + release-macos

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive malformed ELF test suite

Implement Phase 3 Test Coverage requirement: malformed ELF test suite (13 tests).

Test Coverage:
- Invalid ELF magic bytes
- Wrong ELF class (32-bit vs 64-bit)
- Wrong endianness (big-endian)
- Wrong ELF type (ET_EXEC vs ET_DYN)
- Missing PT_LOAD segments
- PT_LOAD with Filesz > Memsz
- PT_LOAD Memsz overflow detection
- Missing PT_DYNAMIC segment
- PT_DYNAMIC with invalid file offset
- Unknown/invalid DynTag handling
- String table validation
- Zero alignment handling
- Corrupted dynamic section

All tests verify parser robustness against corrupted/malformed ELF files.

Tests: 13 functions (exceeds 10+ requirement)
Status: All tests passing, no regressions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): add attach alias, improve empty-session messages, add startup hints (#119)

Improves the CLI experience for session management. Adds `termbeam
attach` as a non-breaking alias for `termbeam resume` to match
tmux/docker conventions. Shows the server URL in empty-session messages
so users can tell the server is reachable. Adds next-step hints after
server and service startup showing available commands.

Closes #118

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Beam your terminal to any device — mobile-optimized web terminal with multi-session support
